note: Sourced Patient-Zero Stage II fallback priors. Calibrated primarily to official US population and public-health data because the current taxonomy uses US-style race/ethnicity, education, occupation, and income categories. Some generation-control fields do not have direct epidemiologic equivalents and are marked in methodology.

methodology.age_strata: Child is approximated as ages 6-17 from Census under-18 minus under-5 share; Adult and Elderly use residual working-age and 65+ shares.
methodology.ethnicity: Mapped to mutually exclusive generation categories using Census QuickFacts race/Hispanic percentages; overlapping Census race/Hispanic categories were normalized for synthetic sampling.
methodology.geography: Census reports 80% urban and 20% rural. Urban is split into Urban (Metropolitan) and Suburban as a generation heuristic within the Census urban share.
methodology.education_level: Calibrated to Census high-school-or-higher and bachelor's-or-higher thresholds; College and Graduate are split within bachelor's-or-higher as a generation heuristic.
methodology.occupation_type: Mapped from BLS major occupational groups plus Census age/labor-force context; Student, Retired, and Unemployed are population-role priors, not occupational categories.
methodology.dietary_pattern: Mapped from CDC fruit/vegetable, sodium, and fast-food indicators. These are behavioral proxies, not diagnostic dietary-pattern measurements.
methodology.communication_style: Generation-control prior only; no official epidemiologic source was used.
methodology.medicine_preference: Modern vs Traditional is proxied by NIH/NCCIH complementary-health-use estimates.

sources.title: U.S. Census Bureau QuickFacts: United States
U.S. Census Bureau 2020 Census Urban Areas Facts
BLS Employment by Major Occupational Group, 2024
CDC MMWR: Adults Meeting Fruit and Vegetable Intake Recommendations, 2019
